note: EU legislation — free reuse with attribution per Decision 2011/833/EU. Measures from Article 21; reporting from Article 23.

controls.title: Proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures
controls.description: Entities shall take appropriate and proportionate technical, operational and organisational measures to manage the risks posed to the security of network and information systems. Measures shall be based on an all-hazards approach.

controls.title: Risk analysis and information system security policies
controls.description: Policies on risk analysis and information system security, covering documentation of the risk management approach, risk appetite, and security objectives.

controls.title: Incident handling
controls.description: Policies and procedures for handling cybersecurity incidents, including detection, response, recovery, and documentation of lessons learned.

controls.title: Business continuity and crisis management
controls.description: Business continuity management, including backup management, disaster recovery, and crisis management during and after cybersecurity incidents.

controls.title: Supply chain security
controls.description: Security in supply chains, including security-related aspects concerning the relationships between each entity and its direct suppliers or service providers.

controls.title: Security in network and information systems acquisition, development and maintenance
controls.description: Security in the acquisition, development and maintenance of network and information systems, including vulnerability handling and disclosure.

controls.title: Policies and procedures to assess cybersecurity risk-management effectiveness
controls.description: Policies and procedures to assess the effectiveness of cybersecurity risk-management measures, including regular reviews and independent audits.

controls.title: Cybersecurity hygiene practices and training
controls.description: Basic cyber hygiene practices and cybersecurity training for all staff, including awareness programs covering phishing, password hygiene, and social engineering.

controls.title: Cryptography and encryption policies
controls.description: Policies and procedures regarding the use of cryptography and, where appropriate, encryption, including key management.

controls.title: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management
controls.description: Human resources security, access control policies and asset management.

controls.title: Multi-factor authentication and secure communications
controls.description: Use of multi-factor authentication or continuous authentication solutions, secured voice, video and text communications and secured emergency communication systems within the entity.

controls.title: Notification of significant incidents to CSIRT
controls.description: Essential and important entities must notify without undue delay their CSIRT or competent authority of any significant incident, and notify recipients of their services where the incident may adversely affect provision of those services.

controls.title: Early warning — within 24 hours
controls.description: Without undue delay, and in any event within 24 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident, submit an early warning to the CSIRT or competent authority indicating whether the incident is suspected to be caused by unlawful or malicious acts and whether it may have a cross-border impact.

controls.title: Significant cyber threat communication to recipients
controls.description: Where a significant cyber threat is identified that could affect the recipients of services, notify those recipients of the threat and any protective or remedial measures they can take.

controls.title: Early warning — within 24 hours
controls.description: Submit an early warning to CSIRT or competent authority within 24 hours of becoming aware of a significant incident, indicating whether the incident is suspected to be caused by unlawful or malicious acts and whether it may have a cross-border impact.

controls.title: Incident notification — within 72 hours
controls.description: Submit incident notification within 72 hours of becoming aware, including initial assessment of severity, impact, and indicators of compromise.

controls.title: Intermediate report (on request)
controls.description: Provide an intermediate status report upon request of the CSIRT or competent authority.

controls.title: Final incident report — within 1 month
controls.description: Submit a final report within one month of the incident notification, including root cause analysis, mitigation measures taken, and cross-border impact.
